title: annonaceae
intro: The rank Annonaceae refers to a family of flowering plants, consisting of approximately 2,000 species within 130 genera. This family is commonly known as the custard apple family, due to the edible fruits of some of its members. The Annonaceae family is characterized by its diverse range of plant forms, including trees, shrubs, and climbing vines, and is found in tropical and subtropical regions around the world. Many species within this family have potential medicinal and nutritional uses, further highlighting their importance and value.
